content: The APOLLO lab has two papers accepted to IROS 2025!
The APOLLO lab has two papers accepted to RSS 2025!

Our work, AI-Powered Semantic Segmentation Model for Enhanced Ureteral Mapping and Real-Time Instrument Feedback in Robotic Surgery, in collaboration with Yale New Haven Health, will be presented at the Korean Society of Endo-Laproscopic & Robotic Surgery (KSERS) Symposium in April 2025.

Our work, AI-Technology Innovations in Robotic Colorectal Surgery – Detecting Optimal Angles for Critical Exposure and Division of the Inferior Mesenteric Artery, in collaboration with Yale New Haven Health, will be presented at the American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons (ASCRS) meeting in San Diego May 2025.
Our work, Sequential Discrete Action Selection via Blocking Conditions and Resolutions with authors Liam Merz Hoffmeister, Brian Scassellati, and Daniel Rakita, will be presented at IROS 2024.
This year's issue of Yale Engineering Magazine highlights a lot of exciting work happening in Yale robotics!
Excited to share that our grant proposal Robot Manipulation in Densely Cluttered Environments was recently funded by the Office of Naval Research.
